survey.title: Gujarat Manufacturing Barometer - Employer Survey
survey.description: Survey for manufacturing employers in Gujarat

questions.text: What is the primary sector of your firm?
questions.trailingText: What is the primary sector of your firm?

questions.text: How many employees does your firm currently have (including contractual)?
questions.trailingText: How many employees does your firm currently have (including contractual)?

questions.text: How long has this plant/unit been operational?
questions.trailingText: How long has this plant/unit been operational?

questions.text: What is the ownership type of this firm?
questions.trailingText: What is the ownership type of this firm?

questions.text: Is this plant located within a designated industrial estate, SEZ, or cluster?

options.label: Yes, in a government-designated industrial estate/GIDC
Yes, in a private industrial park

questions.trailingText: Is this plant located within a designated industrial estate, SEZ, or cluster?

questions.text: What is the primary market orientation of this plant's output?
questions.trailingText: What is the primary market orientation of this plant's output?

questions.text: Where does your product lie on the product technology frontier?

options.label: Similar to other competitors in the region

questions.trailingText: Where does your product lie on the product technology frontier?

questions.text: Where does your process technology (on the shop floor) lie on the process technology frontier?

options.label: Similar to other competitors in the region

questions.trailingText: Where does your process technology (on the shop floor) lie on the process technology frontier?

questions.text: Do you think manufacturing is as or more difficult than any service business?
questions.trailingText: Do you think manufacturing is as or more difficult than any service business?

questions.text: What is your assessment of  ease of doing manufacturing?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Extremely difficult) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Extremely easy)

questions.text: Rank the following on the basis of how important or critical this issue is for the smooth operation of your manufacturing business. In rare cases, you may give more than one indicator the same rank.
questions.trailingText: Rank the following on the basis of how important or critical this issue is for the smooth operation of your manufacturing business. In rare cases, you may give more than one indicator the same rank.

questions.text: Overall, how easy is it for your firm to find workers with the skills it needs?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Extremely difficult) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Extremely easy)

questions.text: How difficult is it to find workers in each of the following categories? [Please tick mark]
questions.trailingText: Role type	Extremely difficult	Difficult	Neutral	Easy	Extremely easy	N/A

questions.text: Where do you typically source workers from? (Select all that apply)
questions.trailingText: Where do you typically source workers from? (Select all that apply)

questions.text: Approximately what share of your current workforce comes from outside Gujarat?
questions.trailingText: Approximately what share of your current workforce comes from outside Gujarat?

questions.text: How has workforce availability changed over the past 3 years?
questions.trailingText: How has workforce availability changed over the past 3 years?

questions.text: How significant a problem is worker attrition for your firm's operations?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Not a problem at all) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Severe, ongoing problem)

questions.text: What is the approximate annual attrition rate at your plant?
questions.trailingText: What is the approximate annual attrition rate at your plant?

questions.text: Which worker categories experience the highest attrition? (Select up to 2)
questions.trailingText: Which worker categories experience the highest attrition? (Select up to 2)

questions.text: What are the primary reasons workers leave? (Select top 3)

options.label: Seasonal / agricultural pull (return to home state/village)
Work perceived as unattractive or low-status

questions.trailingText: What are the primary reasons workers leave? (Select top 3)

questions.text: How significant is competition from other states or sectors in pulling workers away?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Not a problem) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Major ongoing problem)

questions.text: How would you rate the adequacy of your firm's current investment in workforce training?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Very inadequate) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Very adequate)

questions.text: Does your firm have a structured training programme for new joiners?

options.label: Yes, formal structured programme with defined curriculum
No structured training; workers learn as they go

questions.trailingText: Does your firm have a structured training programme for new joiners?

questions.text: How much does your firm invest in training per employee per year (approximate)?
questions.trailingText: How much does your firm invest in training per employee per year (approximate)?

questions.text: Which training providers does your firm use? (Select all that apply)

options.label: We do not use external providers

questions.trailingText: Which training providers does your firm use? (Select all that apply)

questions.text: On average, how long does it take a new hire to become independently productive on your line, by educational background?
questions.trailingText: New hire type	Less than 1 week	1-4 weeks	1-3 months	3-6 months	More than 6 months	We don't hire this type

questions.text: How satisfied are you with the job-readiness of candidates from the following sources?
questions.trailingText: Source	Very Dissatisfied	Dissatisfied	Neutral	Satisfied	Very Satisfied	Don't Use

questions.text: What is the single biggest gap between what educational institutions produce and what your firm needs? (Open-ended, 2-3 lines)
questions.trailingText: What is the single biggest gap between what educational institutions produce and what your firm needs? (Open-ended, 2-3 lines)

questions.text: How well does your firm provide workers with opportunities to grow and advance their careers?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (No real opportunities exist) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Strong, well-defined pathways)

questions.text: Does your firm have defined career progression pathways for shopfloor workers?

options.label: Yes, formal ladders with defined criteria for promotion
Informal — progression happens but is not formally structured

questions.trailingText: Does your firm have defined career progression pathways for shopfloor workers?

questions.text: What share of your supervisory/managerial roles are filled by internal promotion from the shopfloor?
questions.trailingText: What share of your supervisory/managerial roles are filled by internal promotion from the shopfloor?

questions.text: How would you characterise the perception of manufacturing jobs among workers and their families in your experience?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Very negative — seen as low-status, last resort) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Very positive — seen as stable, desirable career)

questions.text: Has your firm taken any deliberate steps to make manufacturing roles more attractive? (Select all that apply)
questions.trailingText: Has your firm taken any deliberate steps to make manufacturing roles more attractive? (Select all that apply)

questions.text: How embedded are structured operational management programmes in your plants (e.g., Lean, TPM, Six Sigma, JIT) in your firm's day-to-day shopfloor operations?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Not adopted at all) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Deeply embedded in how we work)

scale.rightLabel: Deeply embedded in how we work

questions.text: Does your firm have a structured continuous improvement programme?
questions.trailingText: Does your firm have a structured continuous improvement programme?

questions.text: Which of the following operational approaches, systems, or tools does your organization use as part of its continuous improvement efforts? (Select all that apply.)

options.label: ISO 9001 or equivalent quality management system

questions.trailingText: Which of the following operational approaches, systems, or tools does your organization use as part of its continuous improvement efforts? (Select all that apply.)

questions.text: For programmes your firm has adopted, how would you describe the level of actual implementation?

options.label: Adopted on paper / certification only; limited shopfloor change
Partially implemented; some teams follow it, others don't
Broadly implemented; most shopfloor operations reflect it
Deeply embedded; the framework genuinely shapes how decisions are made daily

questions.trailingText: For programmes your firm has adopted, how would you describe the level of actual implementation?

questions.text: Who primarily drives operational improvement initiatives at your firm?

options.label: No one actively drives improvement initiatives

questions.trailingText: Who primarily drives operational improvement initiatives at your firm?

questions.text: When did you start the implementation of these programmes?
questions.trailingText: When did you start the implementation of these programmes?

questions.text: How systematically does your firm track and act on shopfloor performance data?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (No formal tracking) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Rigorous tracking with regular review and action)

scale.rightLabel: Rigorous tracking with regular review and action

questions.text: Which of the following metrics does your firm actively track? (Select all that apply)
questions.trailingText: Which of the following metrics does your firm actively track? (Select all that apply)

questions.text: How frequently are performance metrics reviewed with relevant teams?

options.label: Metrics are collected but not regularly reviewed

questions.trailingText: How frequently are performance metrics reviewed with relevant teams?

questions.text: When performance falls below target, what typically happens?

options.label: Formal root cause analysis and corrective action plan
Issue noted but rarely acted upon systematically

questions.trailingText: When performance falls below target, what typically happens?

questions.text: How would you rate your firm's overall quality management maturity?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (No formal quality system) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Excellent — quality is embedded at every stage of production)

questions.text: What is your firm's approximate defect / rejection rate in current production?
questions.trailingText: What is your firm's approximate defect / rejection rate in current production?

questions.text: Which quality assurance practices does your firm use? (Select all that apply)

options.label: Customer complaint tracking and feedback loops

questions.trailingText: Which quality assurance practices does your firm use? (Select all that apply)

questions.text: Has your firm lost or risked losing a customer or contract due to quality failures in the past 3 years?

options.label: Yes, lost a significant customer / contract
Yes, received serious complaints but retained the customer

questions.trailingText: Has your firm lost or risked losing a customer or contract due to quality failures in the past 3 years?

questions.text: How significant are quality compliance requirements from export markets or large domestic buyers in shaping your quality practices?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Not relevant to us) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Major driver of how we manage quality)

scale.rightLabel: Major driver of how we manage quality

questions.text: How would you rate the overall safety culture at your plant?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Safety is an afterthought / reactive) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Safety is proactive and deeply embedded)

scale.rightLabel: Safety is proactive and deeply embedded

questions.text: How many lost-time injuries (LTIs) did your plant record in the past 12 months?
questions.trailingText: How many lost-time injuries (LTIs) did your plant record in the past 12 months?

questions.text: How frequently does your firm conduct formal safety audits or inspections?

options.label: Only when required by regulation or after an incident

questions.trailingText: How frequently does your firm conduct formal safety audits or inspections?

questions.text: Which safety practices are formally in place at your plant? (Select all that apply)

options.label: Written safety policy communicated to all workers
Regular safety training for all staff
Personal protective equipment (PPE) provision and monitoring

questions.trailingText: Which safety practices are formally in place at your plant? (Select all that apply)

questions.text: How would you characterise worker participation in safety at your plant?

options.label: Workers actively report hazards and near-misses
Workers follow safety rules when supervised

questions.trailingText: How would you characterise worker participation in safety at your plant?

questions.text: How actively does your firm pursue improvements to its production processes (beyond routine maintenance)?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (No active process improvement activity) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Continuous, structured improvement programme)

questions.text: In the past 3 years, has your firm made any significant changes to its production processes? (Select all that apply)

options.label: Introduced a new or significantly improved production method
Reduced material waste or energy consumption through process change
Improved yield or output quality through process redesign
Introduced a new product requiring new process capabilities
No significant process changes in this period

questions.trailingText: In the past 3 years, has your firm made any significant changes to its production processes? (Select all that apply)

questions.text: Where do ideas for process improvement typically come from at your firm? (Select top 3)
questions.trailingText: Where do ideas for process improvement typically come from at your firm? (Select top 3)

questions.text: Does your firm have any formal R&D activity — even if small-scale?

options.label: Yes, dedicated R&D team or function
Yes, informal R&D by engineers alongside production work
No formal R&D, but we adopt innovations developed elsewhere

questions.trailingText: Does your firm have any formal R&D activity — even if small-scale?

questions.text: If you invest in R&D, what fraction of your revenue goes into it?
questions.trailingText: If you invest in R&D, what fraction of your revenue goes into it?

questions.text: Have you filed any patents?
questions.trailingText: Have you filed any patents?

questions.text: If yes, how many patents have you filed in the last 5 years? ______
questions.trailingText: If yes, how many patents have you filed in the last 5 years? ______

questions.text: What is the highest degree of the person who leads R&D in your firm? ________
questions.trailingText: What is the highest degree of the person who leads R&D in your firm? ________

questions.text: What is the single biggest barrier to process innovation at your firm?

options.label: Cost of experimentation and downtime during trials
Short-term production pressure leaves no time for improvement
Insufficient demand signal from customers to justify change

questions.trailingText: What is the single biggest barrier to process innovation at your firm?

questions.text: How much does your firm's location within its current industrial area or cluster actively benefit its operations?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (No tangible benefit from location) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Location is a significant operational advantage)

scale.rightLabel: Location is a significant operational advantage

questions.text: Which of the following benefits, if any, does your firm derive from being located in its current industrial area? (Select all that apply)

options.label: Access to shared infrastructure (testing, logistics, utilities)
Easier access to subcontractors or ancillary units
None — location is incidental to our operations

questions.trailingText: Which of the following benefits, if any, does your firm derive from being located in its current industrial area? (Select all that apply)

questions.text: How would you rate the quality of shared infrastructure in your industrial area?
questions.trailingText: Infrastructure type	Very Poor	Poor	Adequate	Good	Excellent	Not Applicable

questions.text: Does your firm actively collaborate with other firms in the same industrial area?

options.label: Yes, formal collaboration (joint procurement, shared services, industry body participation)
Minimal — we are aware of neighbours but don't collaborate

questions.trailingText: Does your firm actively collaborate with other firms in the same industrial area?

questions.text: What one improvement to your industrial area's infrastructure or ecosystem would most benefit your firm? (Open-ended, 2-3 lines)
questions.trailingText: What one improvement to your industrial area's infrastructure or ecosystem would most benefit your firm? (Open-ended, 2-3 lines)

questions.text: How would you rate the level of physical automation at your plant (sensors, IoT, robotics, mechanised material handling)?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Entirely manual operations) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Highly automated across most production stages)

scale.rightLabel: Highly automated across most production stages

questions.text: Which of the following physical automation technologies does your firm currently use? (Select all that apply)

options.label: Sensors for process monitoring (temperature, pressure, flow)

questions.trailingText: Which of the following physical automation technologies does your firm currently use? (Select all that apply)

questions.text: What share of your production process is automated?
questions.trailingText: What share of your production process is automated?

questions.text: What primarily drove your firm's investments in physical automation? (Select top 2)

options.label: We have not invested in automation

questions.trailingText: What primarily drove your firm's investments in physical automation? (Select top 2)

questions.text: What is the biggest barrier to greater physical automation at your firm?

options.label: Lack of technical expertise to operate and maintain
Production volumes too low to justify automation
Our process is not amenable to automation

questions.trailingText: What is the biggest barrier to greater physical automation at your firm?

questions.text: How would you rate the level of digitalisation in your firm's operations (ERP, MES, digital records, data-driven decision making)?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Paper-based / no digital systems) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Fully integrated digital operations)

questions.text: Which of the following digital systems does your firm currently use? (Select all that apply)

options.label: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP — e.g., SAP, Tally, Zoho)

questions.trailingText: Which of the following digital systems does your firm currently use? (Select all that apply)

questions.text: How integrated are your digital systems with each other?

options.label: Fully integrated — data flows automatically across systems
Partially integrated — some systems connected, manual handoffs elsewhere

questions.trailingText: How integrated are your digital systems with each other?

questions.text: Who manages your firm's digital systems?

options.label: One or two individuals responsible alongside other duties
Managed entirely by external vendor / service provider

questions.trailingText: Who manages your firm's digital systems?

questions.text: What primarily drove your firm's investments in digitalisation? (Select top 2)

options.label: Desire to improve internal visibility and control
We have not invested significantly in digitalisation

questions.trailingText: What primarily drove your firm's investments in digitalisation? (Select top 2)

questions.text: What is the biggest barrier to greater digitalisation at your firm?

options.label: Lack of internal capability to use systems effectively
Data quality and discipline problems (garbage in, garbage out)
Uncertainty about which systems are right for our scale
Our operations are simple enough not to need it

questions.trailingText: What is the biggest barrier to greater digitalisation at your firm?

questions.text: To what extent is your firm actually extracting measurable value from the technology it has deployed?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Technology deployed but little measurable benefit) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Clear, measurable returns from technology investment)

scale.leftLabel: Technology deployed but little measurable benefit
scale.rightLabel: Clear, measurable returns from technology investment

questions.text: For the technologies your firm has deployed, what benefits have you observed? (Select all that apply)
questions.trailingText: For the technologies your firm has deployed, what benefits have you observed? (Select all that apply)

questions.text: How long did it take before your most significant technology investment started delivering visible returns?
questions.trailingText: How long did it take before your most significant technology investment started delivering visible returns?

questions.text: Does your firm collect data from its machines or production systems?

options.label: Yes, data is collected and regularly analysed to make decisions
Yes, data is collected but rarely analysed systematically
Some data is collected informally but not structured
No data collection from machines or production systems

questions.trailingText: Does your firm collect data from its machines or production systems?

questions.text: How would you characterise the gap between your firm's expectations from technology and actual outcomes?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Far exceeded expectations) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Far below expectations)

questions.text: Overall, how significant are the barriers your firm faces to adopting more advanced technology?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (No significant barriers) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Severe barriers preventing meaningful adoption)

questions.text: How significant is each of the following as a barrier to technology adoption at your firm?
questions.trailingText: Barrier	Not a barrier	Minor	Moderate	Significant	Severe

matrix_rows.label: Lack of skilled staff to operate new technology
Lack of vendors / service providers locally
Technology changes too fast to commit
Senior management not convinced of need

questions.text: Has your firm received any government support for technology adoption?

options.label: Yes, financial subsidy or grant (specify scheme if possible: ___)
No, and we did not seek any

questions.trailingText: Has your firm received any government support for technology adoption?

questions.text: If financing were easier to access, how likely would your firm be to invest more in technology in the next 2 years?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Very unlikely — other constraints dominate) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Very likely — finance is the main constraint)

questions.text: How strategically important does your firm consider technology adoption to its competitiveness over the next 5 years?
questions.trailingText: Likert: 1 (Not important — our competitive advantage lies elsewhere) — 2 — 3 — 4 — 5 (Critical — we cannot remain competitive without it)

questions.text: Does your firm have a concrete plan to invest in new technology in the next 2 years?

options.label: Yes, specific investments identified and budgeted
Yes, intention exists but no concrete plan yet
Under consideration but no decision made

questions.trailingText: Does your firm have a concrete plan to invest in new technology in the next 2 years?

questions.text: Which technologies is your firm most likely to invest in over the next 2-3 years? (Select all that apply)

options.label: AI-based quality inspection or process optimisation

questions.trailingText: Which technologies is your firm most likely to invest in over the next 2-3 years? (Select all that apply)

questions.text: What would most accelerate your firm's technology adoption? (Open-ended, 2-3 lines)
questions.trailingText: What would most accelerate your firm's technology adoption? (Open-ended, 2-3 lines)

questions.text: How aware is your firm of emerging technologies (autonomous systems, digital twins, real-time supply chain visibility)?
Does your firm currently use or plan to use AI, machine learning, or predictive analytics in any production or supply chain function?
What is the single biggest constraint on your firm's manufacturing performance today that has not been captured in this survey? (Open-ended)
questions.trailingText: What is the single biggest constraint on your firm's manufacturing performance today that has not been captured in this survey? (Open-ended)

questions.text: What one change — to policy, infrastructure, institutions, or the business environment — would most improve Gujarat's manufacturing ecosystem from your firm's perspective? (Open-ended)
questions.trailingText: What one change — to policy, infrastructure, institutions, or the business environment — would most improve Gujarat's manufacturing ecosystem from your firm's perspective? (Open-ended)
